questions.question: You have to navigate from the 112° radial at 22 DME to the 202° radial at 22 DME. How much shorter is an RNAV direct course compared to a 22 DME arc course?

A localizer signal can be received 18 nm away from the runway threshold. At that distance, you tune the localizer and discover that you are 10° to the right of the course. How far laterally are you from the extended runway centerline?

You depart from Lansing Capital Region Airport (KLAN) and head due west towards a circular field. Today you're planning to practice turns around a point. KLAN is at N42°46'48" W84°35'24" while the field is at N42°46'48" W84°58'24". How far is the circular field from the airport?

You are instructed to fly a holding pattern (a circular racetrack with two 180° turns, a straight inbound and a straight outbound segment). Tracking inbound to a VOR, you reach a point 31 KM away and start the first 180° turn. After the turn, you pass abeam the holding fix traveling outbound and decide to re-center the VOR needle. It now shows a 9° difference from the inbound course. What is the turn radius in the holding pattern?
If you fly a 34 DME arc from the 282° radial clockwise to the 342° radial, how much excess distance did you fly compared to a straight line between the start and end points of the arc?
You cross NIFAA at 5,000'. Flying at 90 KIAS and maintaining a constant descent after crossing NIFAA, what is your glide slope angle in degrees?
questions.context: Use the following information for the remaining 5 questions. The AWOS broadcasts winds 150° at 24 KTS with a temperature of +9°C, Altimeter 29.79" Hg. The fix "NIFAA" is 11.2 nm from the threshold of runway 18. Runway 18 has a magnetic course of 177° with a variation of 5° E and is located at 1,241' MSL. The weather is the same at all altitudes.

questions.question: After flying the approach for 3 miles the course deviation indicates 0.2 nm off course to the right, how many degrees of total correction are needed to intercept the final approach course in 1 nm?
questions.context: Use the following information for the remaining 5 questions. The AWOS broadcasts winds 150° at 24 KTS with a temperature of +9°C, Altimeter 29.79" Hg. The fix "NIFAA" is 11.2 nm from the threshold of runway 18. Runway 18 has a magnetic course of 177° with a variation of 5° E and is located at 1,241' MSL. The weather is the same at all altitudes.

questions.question: While doing those calculations you realize that you are 4 NM left of course. If you have flown 48 NM already how many degrees must you turn to parallel your planned course?
Reference the previous question. If your planned course was 022°, what course do you need to fly to re-intercept 10 NM prior to your destination which is 64 NM away?
questions.context: Referenced question 22: While doing those calculations you realize that you are 4 NM left of course. If you have flown 48 NM already how many degrees must you turn to parallel your planned course?

questions.question: You plan to use a nice and shallow 2.5° descent all the way to your destination. At an altitude of 35,000 feet with a landing elevation of 13 feet, how far out do you need to start your descent with an average ground speed of 358 knots?

(Using the information from the previous question) Unfortunately, ATC keeps you at 35,000 longer than you would like. They allow you to start your descent just 99 nm from your destination. You start down at a 4° angle to recapture your path. How long before you are back on your desired 2.5° glide path?

choices.text: Approximately 44 nautical miles from the destination
Just around 63 statute miles from the destination

questions.correctChoice: Approximately 44 nautical miles from the destination
questions.context: Referenced question 6: You plan to use a nice and shallow 2.5° descent all the way to your destination. At an altitude of 35,000 feet with a landing elevation of 13 feet, how far out do you need to start your descent with an average ground speed of 358 knots?

questions.question: If you are 5° off-course and GPS says that you are 3.4 NM right of course, what is your distance to the fix?

You are on a 10 nautical mile final for runway 23R at 1,500' MSL. There is a bluff a half mile from the threshold at 173' MSL that you want to clear by 50'. In order to maintain a constant glide path throughout your approach, how far from the threshold will you begin your descent and what will the glide slope angle be? (Assume landing on the threshold, and the threshold elevation is 0' MSL.)

The lateral guidance on an instrument landing system is called the localizer. At a particular runway, the radio antenna for the localizer is 50 feet beyond the departure end of the runway. It sends a signal at an optimum angle so the localizer course width is 700 feet at the runway threshold on the approach end. Find the width of the localizer course 3 nm from the threshold of the 8,000-ft runway.

Your company requires the following descent profile: 2,000 fpm from cruise to 10,000' MSL and then a 3.0° slope at 230 KTAS to touchdown. You are cruising at FL230 at 312 KTAS on the 056° course direct to Razorback. ATC instructs you to "cross Razorback at 4,000." How many miles from Razorback should you begin your descent to meet the restriction using the prescribed profile. Winds are a steady 3325 at all altitudes and the temperature is standard.
